article.title: The new AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe.

harvestedTerms.summary: Google announces the European expansion of its AI-powered Google Finance product, featuring AI-driven research queries, advanced charting tools, real-time market data, and live earnings call audio with synchronized transcripts and AI-generated insights. A 'Deep Search' feature for complex financial questions is highlighted as globally available.

article.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity

harvestedTerms.summary: Allen AI (Ai2) releases EMO, a Mixture-of-Experts model trained end-to-end so that modular expert subsets emerge from data (using document-boundary routing constraints) rather than predefined domain labels. The result is a composable model where 12.5% of experts can serve a specific task at near full-model quality. The article is primarily a technical research announcement covering a novel MoE training methodology.

article.title: A quote from New York Times Editors’ Note

harvestedTerms.summary: Simon Willison quotes a New York Times Editors' Note acknowledging that a reporter published an AI-generated summary of a politician's views as a direct quotation. The note is a brief but significant primary-source document illustrating AI hallucination causing real-world journalistic harm.

article.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers

harvestedTerms.summary: A rolling news roundup from The Verge covering data center expansion, energy consumption controversies, environmental impact lawsuits, geopolitical threats, and infrastructure policy debates. The article covers topics from Arm's first CPU to NAACP litigation against xAI to bipartisan energy-usage disclosure efforts.

article.title: “Cyber Defense Has to Move at the Speed of AI”

harvestedTerms.summary: HBR executive newsletter framing AI-speed cyber defense as a strategic leadership concern. The article excerpt is largely paywalled boilerplate with minimal substantive content beyond the headline framing.
